ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-04-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patrick Hall for Claudia Centomini.

Mr. Hall stated that the Order for Final Distribution was an agreement, that Ms. Boessenecker and Mr. Owens signed off on the form of the order because they represent all of the Heirs except for the two Co-Administrators, and that all of the Arizona expenses were being apportioned to the individuals receiving the property. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-22-2010 Continued To Jan-12-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patrick Hall for Claudia Centomini.

Mr. Hall requested a continuance stating that they were waiting to obtain the waiver of accounting from Ms. Boessenecker's Client and that they resolved to issue of the Arizona expenses. The matter was continued to January 12, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-08-2010 Continued To Dec-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patrick Hall for Claudia Centomini.

Mr. Hall requested a continuance stating that he filed waivers of the accounting, that he worked out a compromise with Ms. Boesseneker and her Clients, that Ms. Boessenecker received a waiver from Danny Buth (Heir) but that she had not received one from Patricia Solem (Heir). The matter was continued to December 22, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-17-2010 Continued To Dec-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-26-2010 Continued To Nov-17-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patrick Hall for Claudia Centomini.

Mr. Hall stated that there was a dispute over $15,000.00 and that they were hopeful that they could come to a compromise. The matter was continued to November 17, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-05-2010 Continued To Oct-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patrick Hall for Claudia Centomini.

The Court stated that the issue is who should pay on the Arizona property and that it seems that the heirs are benefiting. Mr. Hall stated that the intestacy in one state is totally different from all other states, that in Arizona the checking account is personal property and goes to the heirs, and that under Arizona law half of the proceeds go the Maternal heirs and the other half goes to the Paternal heirs, that the ruling was made, but that his argument is that it shouldn't be based on this one case. The Court inquired whether the money from the sale of the property was in the bank. Mr. Hall stated that the money had already been distributed, that Ms. Boessenecker's argument shouldn't be according to Arizona law, that there are sufficient funds to cover the $15,000.00, that the Arizona issues were extinguished with the distribution. The Court stated that if certain heirs were benefiting from the property then they should pay those expenses as Judge Ballati found under Arizona law. Mr. Hall asked what if there were four heirs. The Court stated that the benefit would be the same mathematically. The Court questioned why she should be penalized for maintaining the property in Arizona and stated that the costs should be borne by by the heirs who benefited from the Arizona property. Mr. Hall stated that everyone should share the expenses. The Court stated that it thought the costs should come off of the top of the proceeds, that its understanding is that Ms. Boessenecker's clients are prepared to waive the accounting, that Ms. Boessenecker isn't here today, and that it is still missing four waivers. The Court continued the matter to October 26, 2010 and instructed Mr. Hall to contact Ms. Boessenecker to see if they can come to some agreement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: none present.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Continued To Oct-05-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-24-2010 Continued To Sep-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patrick Hall for Claudia Chentomini (Co-Administrator). David Owens for several Heirs of the Estate.

The Court stated that the matter had been continued several times, that the accounting still is not in order, that there were a number of problems with the accounting and asked if there were any thought about the Beneficiaries waiving the accounting. Mr. Owens stated that he could probably get the Beneficiaries to waive the accountings, that he could confer with Janet Boessenceker and that there was only $75,000.00 left in the account. The matter was continued to September 21, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Hall will prepare the order, submit it to Mr. Owens and Ms. Boessenecker, and to the Court.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Distribute Proceeds Form Sale Of Arizona Real Priperty; Declararion Of Pth And Meme Of P'S And A'S In Supprot
Off Calendar Jun-05-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Owens for Michelle Thelen. Patrick Hall for Claudia Centomini.

The Court stated that it was not prepared to make a decision today and that it needed to do additional research. The Court inquired if the property in Arizona is not sold would it pass along. Mr. Owens stated that it would be wise to have the California law preside in this case, that his papers address this issue, that you can distribute under California law, but Arizona law is different. The Court asked counsel if there were no heirs would it escheat to California or Arizona. Mr. Owens stated that would depend if the Arizona Judge felt his/her state needed the money and that the Judge in Arizona sent it back here. In addition, Mr. Owen stated that whether the property was sold or not that California law applies. Mr. Hall stated that he was surprised to find a difference in intestacy law, that the Court was duty bound to apply the law, that he did not know why the Judge did not make a ruling, that there are no beneficiaries in California that would benefit, and that the Arizona laws of intestacy apply. The matter was taken under submission.

Mr. Hall addressed the accounting stating that he was waiting for a ruling on this matter before the accounting would be done and that he estimated that he would have it on file by September 15, 2009.

The Court set the status on the filing of the final accounting for September 15, 2009 and stated no appearances would be necessary if it was on file.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition For Suspension Of Powers And Removal Of Co-Administrator
Off Calendar Jul-23-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Patrick Talbot Hall appearing for Claudia Centomini, co-administrator. Janet Boessenecker appearing for petitioner. Court thought protection was put in place from the last hearing. Ms. Boessenecker argued that Ms. Centomini is going to do whatever it takes to benefit her mother. There is a conflict of interest between the co-administrators. Mr. Hall reported that the bond has been approved. He will send a cashier's check to the Bonding company by tomorrow or Wednesday. Petition is denied without prejudice. With the steps taken in Arizona, Court does not have sufficient ground to remove the co-administrator.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: R. Drocco.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution And For Allowance Of Attorneys' And Administrators' Fees.
Off Calendar Jul-16-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Patrick Talbot Hall appearing for petitioner Claudia Centomini. Janet Boessenecker appearing for Danny Buth. David Ownes appearing for Janet Wall. Ms. Boessenecker has filed a partial opposition to preliminary distribution and for fees. Mr. Hall has filed a reply to the opposition in June. Ms. Boessenecker is concerned about the administrator not getting bond in Arizona. Mr. Hall argued that his client was appointed in Arizona and Ms. Boessenecker should make her objection in Arizona. Ms. Boessenecker has spoken to an attorney in Arizona and was told that it is a different kind of probate proceeding in Arizona and the administrator is not required to give notice until after the fact. The California bond does not cover Arizona property. Her concern is the administrator will distribute more to Lucia than she is entitled to. Witholding Lucia's distribution in California would provide funds for recover of any improper distribution in Arizona. Mr. Hall stated there is sufficient funds left after distribution in California. He stipulated to posting a bond in Arizona. Petition for preliminary distribution is approved with distribution to Lucia and allowance of statutory fees to Mr. Hall and Claudia condition on Mr. Hall filing evidence of filing a bond in Arizona. He will include in his stipulation that notice will be given to parties prior to distribution in Arizona. Mr. Hall requested to dispense with bond. Court instructed him to file an exparte petition for reduction of bond after receipts are filed. Mr. Hall will revise the order and send it to other counsel for approval as to form.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: R. Del Rosario.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution And For Allowance Of Attorneys' And Administrators' Fees.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (199 Cresta Vista Drive, San Francisco Ca)
Off Calendar Apr-04-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Patrick Talbot Hall appearing for co-administrators. Janet Boessenecker appearing for two heirs for the limited purpose to set bond. Sale is confirmed at $735,000.00. Additional bond issue addressed by both counsel. Ms. Boessenecker wants an additional bond of $400,000.00 to cover the Arizona property. Court is setting the additional bond in the amount of $350,000.00. Mr. Hall will submit the order with bond.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: R. Guitron.

He has filed the reappraisal for sale and the waiver of notice from the Tax Collector this morning. The buyer is in court. Publication is not correct giving today's date. Court is shortening time for publication to five days. Matter is continued to 4/4.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Loos.

After brief discussion and noting no objections to the proposed Order, the Court granted the Petition, signing the Order for same (as revised) in open court.

Judicial Officer: Dorothy McMath, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: Katie Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Jimmy Peralta; Court Examiner: None; Court Clerk: R. Michael Diles.
